examples.title: Von Neumann Architecture
examples.description: Demonstrates the fetch-decode-execute cycle in a Von Neumann Architecture
examples.code:
BEGIN
 FETCH instruction from memory
 DECODE instruction
 EXECUTE instruction
 STORE results in memory
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the basic steps involved in executing an instruction in a Von Neumann Architecture.

examples.title: Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP) Architecture
examples.description: Simulates multiple processors executing tasks concurrently in an SMP architecture
examples.code:
import threading

def task(id):
 print(f'Task {id} executed')

# Simulate multiple processors
processors = 4
tasks = 8
threads = []

for i in range(tasks):
 thread = threading.Thread(target=task, args=(i,))
 threads.append(thread)
 thread.start()

for thread in threads:
 thread.join()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates how multiple tasks can be executed concurrently in an SMP architecture using threading.

examples.title: Non-Uniform Memory Access (NUMA) Architecture
examples.description: Simulates processors accessing local and remote memory in a NUMA architecture
examples.explanation: This C code illustrates how processors can access local and remote memory in a NUMA architecture.
